model.label: MiMo-V2-Flash
model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Michigan enters as the superior team by a wide margin in efficiency metrics, ranking 2nd nationally in both KenPom and Torvik with elite defense and a top-10 offense, while Alabama ranks 16th-17th with a potent offense but a significantly weaker defense. The Wolverines' balanced attack and defensive prowess should control the tempo and exploit Alabama's defensive vulnerabilities, especially given the Crimson Tide's recent off-court issues with Aden Holloway's arrest, which could disrupt their backcourt depth and shooting. While Alabama's elite offense gives them a credible upset path if they force turnovers and shoot well, Michigan's superior metrics, depth, and matchup advantage make them the reliable pick to advance.

reasoningStep.title: Investigate Team Quality, Health, and Matchup Dynamics
reasoningStep.summary: I used tools to gather ratings, injury news, and matchup context for Michigan and Alabama before making a pick.
reasoningStep.evidence: Ratings: Michigan ranks 2nd in KenPom/Torvik (adjO 126.7/127.7, adjD 88.4/90.5); Alabama ranks 16th-17th (adjO 129/129.4, adjD 103.4/102.8). Injury/News: Alabama's Aden Holloway arrested for marijuana possession, likely out for tournament; Michigan's Yaxel Lendeborg minor injury from prior round but expected to play. Matchup: Michigan's elite defense should challenge Alabama's offense, while Alabama's weak defense is vulnerable to Michigan's balanced attack. Prior picks show Michigan favored in earlier rounds.

result.text: Can Alabama basketball survive March Madness without Aden Holloway? Skip to main content Home Schedule NFL News Draft Fantasy Odds Betting Newsletter USA TODAY Sports What does Alabama basketball look like without Aden Holloway in March Madness? Emilee Smarr Tuscaloosa News Spirits were low for Alabama basketball coming into the NCAA Tournament after an early exit from the SEC tourney in Nashville. Now, the Crimson Tide carries more heartbreak to Tampa as it stares down March Madness without its top three-point shooter. On Monday, March 16, Alabama guard Aden Holloway was arrested for first-degree possession of marijuana and failure to affix a tax stamp and was transported to the Tuscaloosa County Jail. The University of Alabama has yet to release a statement on Holloway, or his status. However, facing two felony charges, it is hard to see a world in which the junior returns to competition. Not with the Crimson Tide, but possibly ever again. REQUIRED READING: What failure to affix tax

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Tournament year: 2026

Current game label: Midwest Sweet 16 1
Round: Sweet 16
Resolved teams: Michigan (michigan) vs Alabama (alabama)
Prior committed picks: 58
